data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture to decouple services for scalability and flexibility.
data.technical_spec: Individual services with specific functionalities that communicate over HTTP and message queues.

data.title: AWS Integration
data.description: Extensive use of AWS infrastructure to ensure high availability and redundancy.
data.technical_spec: Services like EC2, S3, and DynamoDB are used.

data.title: Monolithic Legacy Support
data.description: Support for legacy systems through monolithic components.
data.technical_spec: Older parts of the system may still require synchronous communication.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Use of AWS Lambda for certain functions to provide scalability without managing infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: Event-driven execution and runs on AWS's shared infrastructure.

data.title: Data-Driven Architecture
data.description: Incorporation of big data and real-time analytics into various services.
data.technical_spec: Tools like Amazon Redshift and Amazon Kinesis are used.
